_comment: Config for Hunter's engage system: target accounts, daily caps, timing, harvest knobs. Swap target_accounts and re-run harvest_replies + engage_daemon to retarget.
_list_url_note: Optional X List URL for efficient polling. If empty, engage_daemon will poll each target profile individually (slower, more requests). Set up via UI or scripts/setup_list.py.
_targets_note: Placeholder list — mix of in-band-likely seeds and oversize-but-real accounts (antirez, MatthewBerman). Replace once scout output is curated.
_archetypes_note: Groups targets with similar reply-culture, so the generator can fall back to archetype-mates when a specific target has few winning-reply examples in the library.

polling._polling_note: Each cycle: target sweep → keyword sweep → sleep.

keyword_engage._keyword_buckets_note: Tier 1 = AI/dev (proven yield 2026-05-15); Tier 2 = GTM/sales agent niche; Tier 3 = GEO/answer-engine optimization; Tier 4 = agent marketing tools. Dropped 'frontier model' + 'context engineering' (zero yield today).
keyword_engage._kw_note: Each cycle picks `keywords_per_sweep` keywords (rotating), searches X, takes top `results_per_keyword`. Wider age filter than target sweep (30 vs 15 min). `skip_targets:true` prevents double-replying to posts by accounts already covered by the target sweep.

quote_scout._comment: Quote-tweet scout — periodic run picks high-engagement posts on configured keywords and drafts QT comments. Stricter than the reply-keyword filter because QTs cost more on Hunter's audience.

ai_news_scout._comment: AI news scout — pulls fresh AI-commentary posts (the high-engagement aggregator/take-maker archetype, not lab orgs) and drafts 3 variants per item (quote_take / original_reframe / counter_take). Hybrid source: account list = curated commentator handles; keyword catch-all = viral posts on AI topics regardless of author. Topped up via TG approve flow.
ai_news_scout._accounts_note: Commentator/aggregator archetype — accounts that take AI news and repackage it as viral threads. Seeded from Hunter's repost (Shelpid_WI3M) + 2 screenshot picks (noisyb0y1, Mnilax) + commentator targets that already appear in target_accounts. The last 3 are educated guesses — if scout logs show 0 candidates from them after a few runs, drop them.
ai_news_scout._filters_note: 100♥ / 24h / 60 chars — commentator accounts post often and the viral ones easily clear 100 likes in a day. min_chars 60 because the screenshot-style threads are always long.
ai_news_scout._variants_note: 1 = one draft per news item, mode rotates across runs (each batch shows a mix). Bump to 3 if you want to A/B compare framings on the same news, but TG gets noisy.

keyword_catchall._comment: If the account-list pool is dry (no qualifying items), run a keyword search and pull viral AI posts regardless of author. This is what catches the @noisyb0y1 / @Mnilax-style viral threads that aren't in our account list.
keyword_catchall._kw_filters_note: Stricter floor (500♥) for keyword catch-all because we have no author quality control. 48h age window — Top-tab viral content typically sits in the 1-3 day band, so 24h misses obvious news.

buildlog._comment: Build-in-public producer — periodic run pulls recent commits and queues original-post drafts via the TG approve flow. Each repo can be local ('path') or remote ('github'). Set GITHUB_TOKEN in .env to raise rate limits and reach private repos.
buildlog._drafts_note: 1 draft/run, spread across day by launchd. buildlog: 09/13/17 SF; quote_scout: 11/15/19/21 SF. Total 7/day.

boost._comment: Boost = hunter's matrix accounts (mguo, shulex) like + retweet + reply when hunter posts. Run: python3 scripts/boost_hunter.py
